themes.mechanism: Two axes: `data-theme` (mode: dark/light, 'system' follows the OS) and `data-palette` (signal hue: tangerine/bubblegum/limeade/crayon, default tangerine) on <html>, both resolved in JS (src/lib/theme.ts) and painted flash-free by a boot script in index.html. The Tailwind semantic colors read CSS variables, so every mode x palette combo flows from one token vocabulary. COLOR-AS-ACCENT doctrine (2026-07-17): the neutral canvas is SHARED by every palette — palette blocks in src/index.css redefine only the accent family (--accent/--accent-ink/--on-accent), the meter, the --row-sel signal wash, and (where the signal collides with deletes) danger. Bevels/glow/motion inherit; the LED glow auto-follows via var(--accent-ink).

tangerine.feel: default; one juicy orange does all the talking, sunshine meter (#ffce33 / #b98600)

bubblegum.feel: hot magenta record-light, aqua meter (#2ee6c8 / #0d9488); danger shifts to plain red (#ff5c5c dark / #b91c1c light) so deletes never read as the pink signal

limeade.feel: lime on dark deepened to emerald in light for AA on white; violet meter (#b497ff / #7c58e8) as the wink

crayon.feel: cobalt signal, green meter (#3ddc74 / #1e9e4e); quietest chrome, the crayon-box note data carries the playfulness. The one palette where --on-accent diverges from --on-bright: the light cobalt fill carries white text

dark.displayName: The Listening Station (untinted graphite; color as accent)

light.displayName: The White Sheet (pure white end to end — page, panels, and masthead; hairlines carry structure; color only as accent)

rules: Accent-not-canvas: surfaces are neutral in both modes (untinted graphite dark, pure white light). Color arrives only as the signal, the meter, the note data, and the faint --row-sel signal wash on the selected row (12-14% over panel in dark, 7-8% over white in light). No other background may carry a hue.

Two signals: `--accent` is the bright signal for FILLS/graphics (Play, spines, dots, progress); `--accent-ink` is the AA-safe variant for TEXT/LED/links/focus. Identical in dark, divergent in light (a fill-strength signal fails AA as text on white).

`--on-bright` is the text that sits ON a data-hue fill (timecode labels): dark in both themes. `--on-accent` is the text on the SIGNAL fill (Play, sign-in, copy): = on-bright everywhere except Crayon light, where the cobalt fill carries white text.
Note/tag/element hues stay raw as FILLS; used as TEXT or a 1px border on the white page they are mixed toward ink for AA (src/lib/noteColors.ts `hueText`).
Every light pair is WCAG AA verified: body/muted/placeholder/accent-ink >= 4.5:1, non-text graphics >= 3:1.

The White-Sheet Rule (2026-07-17, supersedes White-Page + Dark-Masthead): light is one clean white sheet INCLUDING the global header — the `.chrome-dark` dark-masthead zone was retired with the tinted chrome when the user chose color-as-accent-only. The LED glow is dropped in light (a glow is a dark-screen affordance).

display.purpose: The editable track title; the largest prose on screen. A tool, so it never shouts (<=1.125rem).

body.purpose: Note content and general UI copy; IBM Plex Sans (humanist-technical), ~65-75ch measure.

label.purpose: Panel title bars, badges, micro-controls. Monospace, uppercase, +0.2em.

mono.purpose: The transport/header clock and timecode tags. Signal-colored, tabular, glowing in dark.

shadows.name: inset-bevel
shadows.purpose: Recessed screens only: LED readouts, the meter well, inputs, the editor canvas.

shadows.name: raised-bevel
shadows.purpose: The primary Play button only. A hint of a physical key, never a gel button.

motion.name: state-transition
motion.purpose: Default hover/active feedback on controls. Quiet and immediate.

motion.name: seg-flicker
motion.purpose: Segmented LED meter shimmer; staggered per segment to read like a VU meter.

components.name: Play Button
components.description: The one filled control in the transport; toggles playback.

components.name: Capture Button
components.description: Primary creative action: add a note at the current playhead. Signal outline, not a second solid-signal control.

components.name: LED Readout
components.description: Recessed signal-colored clock; the transport and header timecode.

components.name: Panel Title Bar
components.description: The load-bearing instrument-panel header worn by every panel.

components.name: Note Row
components.description: Signature component: a flush cue-list row with a colored identity spine and a mono timecode tag. Not a card.
components.html:
● live
Famous opening motif: short-short-short-long.

components.name: Track Row
components.description: Flush sidebar track entry; active state carries a 2px signal left-bar.
components.html: ▶Beethoven – Symphony No. 5
components.css: .ds-track{display:flex;align-items:center;gap:6px;border-bottom:1px solid rgb(54 54 61 / .4);border-left:2px solid transparent;padding:6px 8px 6px 10px;color:#a4a4ae;font:400 0.875rem 'IBM Plex Sans',system-ui,sans-serif;cursor:pointer}.ds-track:hover{color:#ececf0}.ds-track--active{border-left-color:#ff6a3d;background:#28282d;color:#ececf0}.ds-track-glyph{color:rgb(255 106 61 / .7);font:0.75rem 'IBM Plex Mono',ui-monospace,monospace}.ds-track-name{overflow:hidden;text-overflow:ellipsis;white-space:nowrap}

narrative.overview: Sound Annotator is built like an audio analysis bench, not a web app. The stage is one untinted neutral — quiet graphite in dark, pure white end to end in light — so the eye rests on the recording and the notes, not the chrome. Everything reads like instrument output: timecodes and counts are monospace, the transport carries an LED clock and a level meter, panels are flush with softly squared corners. It is dense by intent, optimized for a teacher who returns daily and knows it cold. Color is accent, never canvas: one playful selectable signal hue (Tangerine default) means 'now', a meter hue supports it, and the crayon-box note palette encodes identity — always paired with a timecode label so meaning survives a projector or a colorblind viewer.
keyCharacteristics: One untinted neutral stage (graphite dark / pure-white light), four-step tonal ramp; depth from tone and hairlines, not shadow.
Color only as accent: one signal hue meaning 'now', a meter hue, and the crayon-box note data. Surfaces never take a tint.
Monospace tabular numerics for every timecode, count, and micro-label.
Softly squared corners (chips 5px / controls 6px / grouped containers 8px / panels 10px), flush panels, hairline dividers; zero card-gaps.
Secondary controls hidden at rest, revealed on hover or focus.

rules.name: The Accent-Not-Canvas Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are neutral in both modes — untinted graphite dark, pure white light. Color arrives only as the signal, the meter, the note data, and the faint --row-sel wash on the selected row. If a background carries a hue anywhere else, it's wrong.

rules.name: The Signal-Is-Now Rule
rules.body: The active palette's signal means the present moment or the primary action, nothing else. Forbidden as a decorative fill. If more than ~10% of a screen is the signal, something non-temporal has stolen it.

rules.name: The Color-Is-Data Rule
rules.body: Hue only encodes identity (which note), never emotion or hierarchy. Any color-coded element must also carry a text label (timecode), so it reads on a dim projector and for colorblind users.

rules.name: The Monospace-Numerics Rule
rules.body: Every timecode, duration, and count is monospace and tabular-figure; numbers never reflow. Prose is never monospace; numerics are never proportional.

rules.name: The No-Shouting Rule
rules.body: Product UI: display type stays <=1.25rem. No hero type. Hierarchy comes from weight, case, and the mono/sans split, not size.

rules.name: The Tonal-Depth Rule
rules.body: Reach for the surface ramp before any shadow. To separate two surfaces, change tone or draw a hairline; do not float one on a shadow.

rules.name: The Bevel-Restraint Rule
rules.body: Bevels are 1px lighting hints on interactive controls only. Textures, surface gradients, gloss, and faux-material finishes are forbidden. If a bevel looks like brushed metal, it has failed.

dos: Do build depth from the tonal ramp (ink -> panel -> raised -> inset) and hairline dividers. Flush panels, softly squared corners (5-10px).
Do keep numerics monospace and tabular: timecodes, counts, durations.
Do keep the canvas neutral and reserve the signal for 'now' and the primary action; keep it under ~10% of any screen.
Do pair every color-coded note with its timecode label, so identity never depends on hue alone (projector + colorblind safe).
Do hide secondary controls until hover/focus; let the resting UI stay calm.
donts: Don't ship the generic AI / shadcn dashboard look: rounded cards, soft drop shadows, indigo accent, evenly padded 'friendly SaaS' whitespace.
Don't drift into heavy enterprise SaaS clutter: gray-on-gray toolbars, competing controls, data-grid busyness.
Don't use retro skeuomorphism: fake wood or metal textures, glossy bevels, brushed-aluminum.
Don't let color carry meaning alone, and don't add a second face to the type system. Two voices (sans + mono) only.
Don't introduce a second solid-signal control next to Play, tint a surface with the signal (beyond --row-sel), or round a corner past the lg=10px panel radius.
